title: CATL secures safety production permit for Jianxiawo lithium mine
gist: CATL has secured the safety production permit needed to restart its idle Jianxiawo lepidolite lithium mine in Jiangxi, China; Benchmark forecasts 37kt LCE from the mine in 2026 on a September restart, with an earlier restart increasingly likely given rapid recent approvals.

insights.claim: CATL can likely restart its Jianxiawo lepidolite lithium mine in Jiangxi, China after securing a safety production permit that Benchmark has seen a copy of, which was considered the last official hurdle to resuming production at a mine idle since August 2025.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Confirms the final regulatory blocker to renewed supply is cleared; a lead indicator for added Chinese lithium output.
insights.quote: Benchmark has seen a copy of the permit certificate, which was considered the last official hurdle CATL needed to overcome to resume production at the mine which has been idle since August 2025.

insights.claim: Benchmark's Q2 Lithium Forecast anticipates 37kt lithium carbonate equivalent to be mined from Jianxiawo in 2026 assuming a September restart, accounting for roughly 1.5% of global mined lithium supply.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Quantifies the restart's contribution to global supply and anchors a baseline model for the year.
insights.quote: Benchmark’s Q2 Lithium Forecast anticipates 37kt lithium carbonate equivalent to be mined from Jianxiawo in 2026 assuming a September restart, accounting for ~1.5% of global mined lithium supply.

insights.claim: Jianxiawo's 2026 production figure could rise above 37kt LCE if the mine restarts earlier than expected, something Benchmark deems increasingly likely given the rapid pace of approvals in the past week.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Upside risk to supply forecasts; accelerating approvals signal Chinese authorities are easing the earlier crackdown.
insights.quote: This production figure could increase if the mine is restarted earlier than expected, something that is increasingly likely given the rapid pace of approvals in the past week.

insights.claim: The Jianxiawo mine is anticipated to operate at its planned capacity of 150ktpa by 2029.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Signals a multi-year ramp to full capacity, growing the mine's share of global lithium supply through the decade.
insights.quote: The mine is anticipated to operate at its planned capacity of 150ktpa by 2029.
insights.locator: The mine is anticipated to operate
